creator: kevans@freebsd.org
text:
Created attachment 179173
Proposed patch to address line matching issues

The failure in bug #181263 case 1 also ended up being fairly straightforward to address -- pmatch gets clobbered by a non-matching case and ended up with bogus values, so now we look at the last *actual match* (if st isn't advancing) and see if that ends up being a zero-length match.

This catches corner cases like the one found where .* can infinitely match but the 'a' pattern comes second. It shouldn't affect any legitimate cases, since those will cause an advancement in nst prior to this.
